rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Blockfrost is the number-one hosted platform used by Cardano developers and survey adoption rose from 39.3% in 2022 to 71.5% in 2025." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence; Provide independent evidence for: In most Cardano epochs, more than 50% of all transactions are submitted through Blockfrost. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score 0.20; confidence 74.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Treasury evidence is not sufficient for YES and does not affirmatively prove waste; requesting the missing evidence instead of defaulting to NO.
top_fixes: Provide: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence
Provide independent evidence for: In most Cardano epochs, more than 50% of all transactions are submitted through Blockfrost.
Provide independent evidence for: More than 100 Icebreakers have joined the decentralized Blockfrost operator effort.
missing_evidence: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Transition Blockfrost into a free, community-governed public good under an independent not-for-profit, funding 18 months of transition and operations.
Recipient: Input Output / Blockfrost transition structure, with Intersect/CDH contract administration described in the document

Deliverables: Establish or host a not-for-profit entity and kick off transition in Q3 2026, Publish technical transition architecture and migration plan for community review, Launch a public usage dashboard, Hold an on-chain board election under published rules by Q4 2026, Serve all public API traffic for mainnet, preview, and preprod from the new stack by Q1 2027, Legally transfer Blockfrost source code, trademarks, domains, and associated assets to the governing entity, Maintain service performance through cutover, Open public sustainability consultation and publish a long-term sustainability proposal in Q2-Q3 2027, Operate the public API at minimum 99% monthly uptime with quarterly technical and budget reports
Deadline/expiry: 18-month transition and operations period, with milestones from Q3 2026 through 2027
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Blockfrost is the number-one hosted platform used by Cardano developers and survey adoption rose from 39.3% in 2022 to 71.5% in 2025.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Blockfrost served 781k unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data last month.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): In most Cardano epochs, more than 50% of all transactions are submitted through Blockfrost.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): More than 100 Icebreakers have joined the decentralized Blockfrost operator effort.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Ninety percent of Cardano traffic served through the Blockfrost API comes from the free tier, making the current commercial model difficult to use for financing the public-good operation.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal will transfer Blockfrost source code, trademarks, domains, and associated assets into community stewardship under a community-governed not-for-profit.
missing: Independent evidence for: Blockfrost served 781k unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data last month.
Independent evidence for: In most Cardano epochs, more than 50% of all transactions are submitted through Blockfrost.
Independent evidence for: More than 100 Icebreakers have joined the decentralized Blockfrost operator effort.

Independent evidence for: Ninety percent of Cardano traffic served through the Blockfrost API comes from the free tier, making the current commercial model difficult to use for financing the public-good operation.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Blockfrost is the number-one hosted platform used by Cardano developers and survey adoption rose from 39.3% in 2022 to 71.5% in 2025." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Blockfrost served 781k unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data last month." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
missing: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: No links to forum discussion or community feedback
Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, whose voting CLOSED with a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.

pending_candidate.note: Voting on this action is still open; its interim tally does not set the operative ceiling. It becomes the pin only if it closes with Yes > No.
